title: Catalog Verification TODO
description: One entry per pattern in patterns[], one entry per composition (recipe or system) in compositions[]. Tracks per-aspect verification status so contributors (and agents) can work through the catalog item-by-item. Aspect values: 'todo' (not yet checked), 'pass' (verified correct), 'fail' (verified wrong, needs fix), 'na' (does not apply). Top-level 'verified' is true only when every applicable aspect is pass or na.

patterns.intent_one_sentence: intent reads as exactly one sentence under 35 words
patterns.context_clear: context is plain English a developer new to the area can follow
patterns.problem_clear: problem clearly states what fails without this pattern
patterns.solution_clear: solution describes the mechanism without recourse to undefined jargon
patterns.constrains_is_restriction: constrains slot reads as a restriction (forbids/must/only) not a benefit
patterns.example_scenario_present: example_scenario field is populated with a plain-English narrative
patterns.diagram_present: diagram.mermaid renders to a meaningful sequence/flow/class/state diagram
patterns.applicability_present: applicability.use_when and do_not_use_when bullets are populated
patterns.variants_present: variants[] populated where the pattern has known implementation shapes
patterns.known_uses_live: every known_uses URL is alive and points to the named system
patterns.no_jargon_undefined: any acronym used in prose is defined in glossary.json or expanded in-place
patterns.edges_correct: every related[] edge points at a real pattern; symmetric/inverse pairs balance

compositions_recipe.description_present: description slot has at least 30 characters of plain-English prose
compositions_recipe.members_min_two: members[] has at least two patterns
compositions_recipe.pattern_ids_resolve: every members[].pattern resolves to an id in patterns-src/
compositions_recipe.roles_valid: every members[].role is one of core / hardening / optional
compositions_recipe.instantiating_systems_present: at least one kind=system composition lists this recipe in its instantiates[] array
compositions_recipe.status_in_practice_set: status_in_practice field is set to one of mature / emerging / experimental / deprecated
compositions_recipe.architectural_diagram_present: pattern_composition holds a Mermaid diagram that visualises the recipe's agent-pattern flow

compositions_framework.vendor_correct: vendor field names the actual maintainer
compositions_framework.language_correct: language field reflects the system's primary languages
compositions_framework.intent_present: intent slot reads as a single sentence stating what the system accomplishes
compositions_framework.description_present: description slot has 2-4 plain-English sentences naming the system, its maker, and its agent-loop shape
compositions_framework.members_have_evidence: every members[].evidence array carries at least one citation (doc/repo/blog/talk) with a working url and a quoted excerpt
compositions_framework.members_accurate: each members[] role reflects current upstream docs; no inflated role labels
compositions_framework.no_missing_pattern_support: patterns the system clearly supports are not omitted from members[]
compositions_framework.last_analyzed_within_quarter: last_analyzed is within the last 90 days
compositions_framework.status_in_practice_set: status_in_practice field is set to one of mature / emerging / experimental / deprecated
compositions_framework.primary_use_cases_present: primary_use_cases array is populated with at least one concrete noun phrase
compositions_framework.architectural_diagram_present: pattern_composition holds a Mermaid diagram that visualises the actual agent-pattern flow (state, control, or component graph), not generic decoration

notes.text: Source-verified against https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/stable/ and tutorial/teams. url_live: 200; content names AutoGen, Microsoft. Vendor Microsoft confirmed. Python primary (3.10+), .NET listed as separate doc nav - language field correct. Coverage cells confirmed: autogen-conversational, supervisor (SelectorGroupChat), orchestrator-workers (MagenticOneGroupChat), tool-use, code-execution (DockerCommandLineCodeExecutor), handoff (Swarm + HandoffMessage), event-driven-agent (core is event-driven by design), step-budget (max_turns on teams), structured-output (output_content_type per AssistantAgent docs). subagent-isolation 'limited' correct: isolation requires separate runtime/process configs. agent-resumption 'limited' correct and source-verified: AutoGen has save_state/load_state but persistence is user-managed (per docs). camel-role-playing 'limited' is correct (no first-class CAMEL primitive; can be assembled via two AssistantAgents in RoundRobinGroupChat). Cell needing correction: code-as-action 'fully' - AutoGen's PythonCodeExecutionTool / CodeExecutorAgent supports code-as-action, but it's not the same as CodeAct (executable Python actions per turn). Borderline; likely keep 'fully' but could be 'limited'. No obvious missing patterns; coverage is well-scoped.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://github.com/yoheinakajima/babyagi. URL HTTP 200; vendor Yohei Nakajima confirmed; language Python 66.5% confirmed. CELL CORRECTION NEEDED: 'react: fully' is inaccurate — the original 2023 BabyAGI used a task-list loop (execution_agent, task_creation_agent, prioritization_agent), which is task-list-driven planning, not classic ReAct (Thought->Action->Observation). The current functionz framework is a function-graph execution model, not ReAct. Recommend dropping the 'react' cell or downgrading to 'limited' with a note that it applies only to the archived legacy script. todo-list-driven-agent: fully and tool-use: limited remain accurate. Possible additions: scheduled-agent (trigger system for automated workflows in functionz), self-archaeology (self_build agent generates new functions that operate on its own function graph). Other 5 aspects pass.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://www.browserbase.com/ (HTTP 200; landing page matches Browserbase) and https://docs.browserbase.com/. Vendor 'Browserbase' confirmed. Language 'API' is correct (Browserbase is delivered as a hosted browser-infrastructure API + SDKs in multiple languages, not as a client-side framework). Coverage cells all confirmed: browser-agent (entire premise - 'Real browsers for your agent to use the web'), sandbox-isolation ('Scalable, sandboxed environments for agent deployments'), session-isolation (Browser API: 'Create, control, and observe browser sessions programmatically' - each session is isolated). MISSING patterns the project clearly supports: 'computer-use' (real browsers with vision/click/type via Stagehand SDK is essentially the same surface as Anthropic Computer Use), 'tool-use' (Stagehand exposes act/observe/extract as agent-callable tools; plus dedicated Search API + Fetch API tools), 'multi-llm-routing' (Model Gateway routes 100+ LLM providers behind a single API key), 'replay-time-travel' ('replays' explicitly mentioned in observability), 'agent-resumption' (sessions can be resumed; contexts persist auth state). Strongest misses: computer-use, multi-llm-routing.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://burr.dagworks.io/ (HTTP 200) and https://burr.dagworks.io/concepts/. Vendor 'DAGWorks' confirmed (project is now also Apache Burr Incubating; DAGWorks remains the originator). Python language confirmed. Burr is fundamentally a state-machine framework — 'elegant yet comprehensive state management' — not an agent framework with built-in tool/ReAct primitives. Coverage cell concerns: 'tool-use': 'fully' is OVERSTATED — Burr does not provide tools as a first-class concept; tools are user-implemented inside Action functions. Suggested correction: 'limited'. 'react': 'fully' is OVERSTATED for the same reason — ReAct is implementable on top of state+actions+transitions but is not a built-in pattern. Suggested correction: 'limited'. 'agent-resumption': 'fully' confirmed (state persistence, snapshots, save/load). 'decision-log': 'fully' is defensible — every state transition is tracked. 'step-budget': 'fully' is OVERSTATED — not a documented first-class concept; users implement via custom transition conditions. Suggested correction: 'limited' or 'unknown'. 'approval-queue': 'limited' is fine (implementable via a 'pause' action + transition condition, but not first-class). MISSING patterns Burr clearly supports: 'replay-time-travel' (explicit replay capability for debugging and eval), 'cost-observability' / 'lineage-tracking' (telemetry + OpenTelemetry + tracking server), 'event-driven-agent' (hooks + parallelism), 'spec-driven-loop' (Hamilton integration). Strongest miss: replay-time-travel.
notes.author: Marco Nissen

notes.text: Applied corrections from prior verification: downgraded tool-use, react, step-budget from fully->limited (Burr is a state-machine framework, these arent first-class); added replay-time-travel=fully, cost-observability=limited, lineage-tracking=limited, event-driven-agent=limited, spec-driven-loop=limited. Verified again.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://github.com/openai/codex (HTTP 200) and https://developers.openai.com/codex. Vendor 'OpenAI' confirmed. Language 'Rust, TypeScript' matches GitHub stats (Rust 96.3% / Python 2.7% / TypeScript 0.3%; TS lives in legacy `codex-cli` JS surface and IDE extensions). Coverage cell concerns: 'computer-use': 'fully' is OVERSTATED for the openai/codex repo. The CLI is terminal-only — it does not drive a browser or screen. The hosted ChatGPT Codex agent (separate product, web/cloud) does have computer-use, but this matrix entry's URL points at the openai/codex CLI repo. Suggested correction: 'none' or 'limited' (limited if you count the IDE-extension UI surface). Other cells confirmed: tool-use (built-in tools + custom in AGENTS.md), code-as-action (files + shell), code-execution (runs commands), approval-queue (suggest/auto-edit/full-auto modes documented), agent-computer-interface (terminal + VS Code/Cursor/Windsurf extensions). MISSING patterns the project clearly supports first-class: 'mcp' (MCP support documented at developers.openai.com/codex), 'agent-resumption' (--resume flag documented), 'plan-and-execute' (plan mode documented), 'agent-skills' / 'skill-library' (AGENTS.md is the canonical custom-agent definition surface), 'sandbox-isolation' (docs/sandbox.md and security docs at developers.openai.com/codex/security), 'profiles' as a configuration pattern (custom profiles documented), 'event-driven-agent' (GitHub/Slack/Linear integrations trigger sessions). Strongest misses: mcp, agent-resumption, plan-and-execute, sandbox-isolation, AGENTS.md / skill-library.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://docs.crewai.com/introduction, /concepts/processes, /concepts/agents, /mcp/overview. url_live: 200. Vendor 'CrewAI Inc.' matches docs/landing page. Python confirmed (only language). Coverage cells accurate: supervisor (hierarchical process with manager_llm/manager_agent), orchestrator-workers (Crews + Flows), tool-use (tools=[...] on Agent), handoff (allow_delegation=True between agents), step-budget (max_iter on Agent, default 20), structured-output (Pydantic output), mcp (FULLY supported per /mcp/overview - 'mcps' field on agents, MCPServerAdapter, stdio/SSE/HTTPS transports). agentic-rag 'limited' is correct (RAG is via tools/knowledge sources, not first-class). camel-role-playing 'limited' is correct (no first-class primitive). Missing patterns to add: 'role-assignment' (CrewAI's defining feature - role/goal/backstory per agent), 'memory' / cross-session-memory (CrewAI has Memory module), 'event-driven-agent' (CrewAI Flows have @start/@listen event triggers), 'agent-resumption' (Flow state persistence). Strongest miss: role-assignment - defining CrewAI feature absent from coverage.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://www.genspark.ai/ (HTTP 403 - accepted per liveness rule, content blocked by anti-bot) and https://mainfunc.ai/ (HTTP 200 - vendor site). Vendor MainFunc confirmed (founded 2024, Palo Alto). "Web product" language designation correct (closed-source consumer/web product, no public SDK). Coverage cells accurate: tool-use (80+ toolkits), agentic-rag (Deep Research processes 1.6M words from 1,338 sources), browser-agent (Genspark Claw 2026 launch - cloud-sandbox autonomous browser). MISSING patterns the product clearly supports: "orchestrator-workers" / "mixture-of-agents" (Genspark explicitly markets "9 specialised LLMs orchestrated by a central orchestrator" - the defining architectural feature), "plan-and-execute" (Super Agent "plans the steps, picks the right tools"), "multilingual-voice-agent" (Call-For-Me autonomously makes phone calls), "code-execution" (Claw provides cloud sandbox to execute tasks across software interfaces), "supervisor" (orchestrator-supervisor pattern). Strongest misses: orchestrator-workers and multilingual-voice-agent (Call-For-Me).
notes.author: Marco Nissen

notes.text: Source-verified against https://haystack.deepset.ai/ (HTTP 200), https://docs.haystack.deepset.ai/docs/agent, https://docs.haystack.deepset.ai/docs/mcptool, https://haystack.deepset.ai/integrations/mcp. Vendor deepset (deepset GmbH) confirmed. Python confirmed (pip install haystack-ai). Coverage cells INACCURATE - several "limited" ratings should be "fully": (1) tool-use rated "limited" but Haystack 2.x has first-class Tool / ComponentTool / Toolset and ToolInvoker primitives - "fully" idiomatic. (2) react rated "limited" but the Agent component IS a loop-based ReAct implementation with tool selection, exit conditions, and streaming callbacks - "fully". (3) step-budget rated "limited" but max_agent_steps is a documented first-class Agent parameter - "fully". (4) structured-output rated "limited" is defensible since state_schema is more stateful than typical structured output. MISSING patterns: "mcp" (Haystack has a dedicated MCP integration - MCPTool + Hayhooks-as-MCP-server, both first-class - "fully" capability completely absent from coverage), "streaming" (streaming_callback on Agent), "memory" (cross-session memory via state_schema/conversational memory components). Strongest miss: mcp.
notes.author: Marco Nissen

notes.text: Applied corrections from prior verification: upgraded tool-use, react, step-budget limited->fully (first-class in Haystack 2.x); added mcp=fully, cross-session-memory=limited; streaming pattern not in catalog (skipped). Verified again.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://platform.hume.ai/docs/empathic-voice-interface-evi (307 -> dev.hume.ai, HTTP 200) and https://www.hume.ai/empathic-voice-interface plus blog/launch posts. Vendor Hume AI confirmed. Languages: TypeScript, Python, REST/WebSocket all documented (Next.js, TypeScript, Python quickstarts). Coverage cells accurate: multilingual-voice-agent (EV4-mini Jan 2026 added 11 languages: English, Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, Portuguese, Italian, German, Russian, Hindi, Arabic - first-class), tool-use (webhooks + tool use to call APIs/databases - documented). MISSING patterns the product clearly supports: "agent-resumption" (EVI supports resumable conversations - chat-history persistence + chat_id continuation), "voice-agent" (general voice-agent pattern beyond multilingual is the primary product), "interruption-handling" / turn-taking (EVI has prosody-aware turn management - core differentiator), "structured-output" (EVI returns structured emotional-prosody scores per utterance - distinctive structured channel), "speech-to-speech" (EVI 2/3 is end-to-end S2S, distinct from ASR+TTS pipelines). Strongest miss: agent-resumption (resumable chats are a documented EVI capability).
notes.author: Marco Nissen

notes.text: Source-verified against https://docs.langchain.com/ and https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/. url_live: 200, content names LangChain. Vendor LangChain Inc. matches site footer. Languages Python+TypeScript confirmed (Go/Java SDKs are LangSmith client-only, not the framework). Coverage cells accurate for: tool-use, react, plan-and-execute, structured-output (response_format with ToolStrategy/ProviderStrategy), agentic-rag, hybrid-search, cross-encoder-reranking, fallback-chain (.with_fallbacks), step-budget (iteration limit per docs), session-isolation, mcp (MCP Toolbox + tools discovered from MCP server). Cells likely needing correction: skill-library currently 'limited' but LangChain has no first-class skill-library primitive (deepagents is closer); should consider 'none' or keep 'limited' if interpreting prompt templates as skills. supervisor 'limited' is correct (delegated to langgraph-supervisor). handoff 'limited' is correct (delegated to langgraph). approval-queue 'limited' is correct (interrupt is a langgraph primitive, not langchain core). citation-streaming 'limited' is reasonable (langchain has streaming but no first-class citation type). context-window-packing 'limited' is reasonable (no first-class compactor in langchain v1). chain-of-thought 'limited' and self-refine 'limited' are reasonable (these are prompting patterns, not langchain primitives). Borderline: contextual-retrieval is 'limited' but LangChain has Anthropic ContextualEmbeddings recipe in docs - keep limited (recipe-level). No obvious missing patterns found beyond what's covered.

Vendor 'LlamaIndex Inc.' matches docs site. Python+TypeScript (LlamaIndex.TS) confirmed. notes.text: Source-verified against https://temporal.io/. url_live: 200. Vendor 'Temporal Technologies' confirmed. Languages confirmed via SDK selector on landing page: Go, Java, Python, TypeScript, .NET (C#), PHP, Ruby. Coverage cells all 'fully' and source-verified: agent-resumption (durable execution - 'workflows automatically capture state at every step, and in the event of failure, can pick up exactly where they left off'), scheduled-agent (workflow.sleep + Schedules API for cron), event-driven-agent (signals + task queues), compensating-action (Saga pattern - 'what if Saga was simply a try...catch?'), step-budget (activity retries with timeouts, retry policies). Missing patterns to add: 'agent-resumption' is covered but 'session-isolation' should be added (per-workflow-id isolation), 'replan-on-failure' (Temporal supports continueAsNew for replan-style behavior), 'tool-use' (Activities are conceptually tools - first-class), 'fallback-chain' (retry policies + activity options). Strongest miss: tool-use and session-isolation since both are foundational primitives.
notes.author: Marco Nissen

notes.text: Applied corrections from prior verification: added tool-use=fully (Activities), session-isolation=fully, replan-on-failure=limited (continueAsNew), fallback-chain=fully (retry policies). Verified again.

The old coverage_cells_accurate aspect dropped (no more coverage matrix); replaced by members_have_evidence which requires each member to carry a verifiable upstream citation. intent_present, description_present added. All new aspects reset to todo regardless of prior verified state, because the data model the prior checks ran against no longer exists.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://cloud.google.com/products/agent-builder, en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vertex_AI. url_live: 200. Vendor 'Google Cloud' confirmed. Language 'API (multi-language SDKs)' accurate — supports open-source Agent Development Kit (ADK; Python, Java) and other frameworks per Wikipedia. Vertex AI Agent Builder = Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (rebranded April 2025+). Coverage cells confirmed: tool-use (ADK tool primitives), agentic-rag (Vertex AI Search built-in), supervisor (ADK supports multi-agent orchestration), structured-output (Gemini structured output API), agent-resumption (Vertex AI Agent Engine provides durable infrastructure for deploying and scaling agents). last_analyzed bumped to 2026-05-02. No obviously missing patterns; could add 'mcp' since ADK supports MCP servers, but kept conservative.

notes.text: Source-verified against https://www.getzep.com/. url_live: 200. Vendor 'Zep AI' (Zep Software, Inc.) confirmed. Language 'Python, TypeScript' accurate (also Go is supported per docs). Coverage cells confirmed: cross-session-memory ('persistent context that captures state changes while integrating new data; provenance so agents can reason with evolving context over time'), five-tier-memory-cascade (Zep historically documented a multi-tier memory architecture; current Graphiti-based implementation uses temporal context graph with episodic/entity/community/edge tiers — 'fully' is defensible but cell label is slightly stale relative to current Graph RAG terminology), session-isolation (per-user session scoping is foundational to Zep's user/session/thread model). last_analyzed bumped to 2026-05-02. No obviously missing patterns; agentic-rag could be added since Zep now exposes Graph RAG explicitly, kept conservative.

consumer_requirements.description: The compositions UI (e.g. http://localhost:8097/compositions/) must offer a filter on status_in_practice so a reader can narrow to ★★ mature, ★ emerging, · experimental, or ✗ deprecated entries.

The compositions and patterns UIs should render last_updated as a relative 'X days ago' string so readers can see at a glance how fresh an entry is. Comparison against verification.last_verified additionally reveals whether verification is stale.

The compositions UI must render two evidence glyphs per entry: (1) a top-level verification_status glyph — ✓ verified / ◐ partial / ○ stub / ⚠ needs-verification — shown next to the composition name; and (2) a per-member evidence_status glyph — ✓ full / ◐ limited / ○ none — shown next to each pattern claim. Both fields are populated automatically by Phase 2; the UI just renders the glyph mapping.

The compositions UI must surface BOTH verification flows distinctly: (a) aspect-driven verification — show the per-aspect statuses produced by Phase 2 (url_live, intent_present, members_have_evidence, etc.) and the top-level verification_status glyph; (b) composition-by-composition quality run — show the quality_review.status (pending / in-progress / passed / failed / stale) plus reviewer + last_run + summary. The two flows are independent: an entry can pass aspect verification but still be 'pending' in the quality run, and vice versa. A quality_review is automatically marked 'stale' when the source composition's last_updated is newer than the review's last_run.
